schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Differential Geometry of Rotating Systems: ds^2

theory.canonical_statement: The mathematical framework requires sophisticated differential geometric machinery. For a rotating spheroid, the metric takes the form: \begin{equation} ds^2 = -c^2\left(1 - \frac{2GM}{rc^2} - \frac{\Omega^2r^2\sin^2\theta}{c^2}\right)dt^2 + \frac{dr^2}{1-\frac{2GM}{rc^2}} + r^2(d\theta^2 + \sin^2\theta\,d\phi^2) \end{equation}
theory.technical_interpretation: Differential Geometry of Rotating Systems: ds^2 is a metric / line element, written ds^2 = -c^2 (1 - (2GM)/(rc^2) - (Ω^2r^2sin^2θ)/(c^2) )dt^2 + fracdr^21-(2GM)/(rc. It specifies a spacetime line element, encoding how distances and causal structure are measured. It is built from Ω, θ, φ, c, G, M, defining ds^2. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
